detaileddescription.para: Mutex used to lock maps while we add to or retrieve from cache
Length of time in seconds that a user will stay in the cache without being accessed before being purged. Defaults to 600 seconds, or 10 minutes.
User cache that maps AccelByte IDs to shared user instances
User cache that maps platform type and ID to shared user instances. The key is just a string that combines both type and ID, in the following format: "TYPE;ID".
AccelByte online subsystem instance that owns this user cache.
Convenience method to get an instance of this interface from the subsystem passed in.

parameterdescription.para: Subsystem instance that we wish to get this interface from
Instance of the interface that we got from the subsystem, or nullptr if not found

simplesect.para: boolean that is true if we could get an instance of the interface, false otherwise

detaileddescription.para: Convenience method to get an instance of this interface from the subsystem associated with the world passed in.

parameterdescription.para: World instance that we wish to get the interface from
Instance of the interface that we got from the subsystem, or nullptr if not found

simplesect.para: boolean that is true if we could get an instance of the interface, false otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (int32 LocalUserNum, const TArray< FString > &AccelByteIds, const FOnQueryUsersComplete &Delegate, bool bIsImportant=false)
memberdef.name: QueryUsersByAccelByteIds

detaileddescription.para: Queries all of the IDs listed in the array on the AccelByte backend for user information, including platform IDs. If platform IDs are found and match the current platform that we are on, extra queries will be made through the platform OSSes.
Caches any results that we get from the backend, as well as will not query from the backend again if a duplicate is found.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the user that is attempting to query for other users
Array of strings that represent an ID for a single user
Delegate fired when the query is complete

Whether or not we want to mark these users as important so that they stay in the cache, defaults to false. This should only be used for users that we want to persist for the length of the game session, such as friends.

memberdef.argsstring: (int32 LocalUserNum, const FString &PlatformType, const TArray< FString > &PlatformIds, const FOnQueryUsersComplete &Delegate, bool bIsImportant=false)
memberdef.name: QueryUsersByPlatformIds

detaileddescription.para: Tries to query all platform IDs listed for the particular platform specified on the AccelByte backend to find AccelByte user matches. If a matches are found, a subsequent query and cache will be performed to get information from the AccelByte backend on those users.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the user that is attempting to query for other users
String representing the type of platform that these IDs belong to
Array of strings that represent an ID for a single user
Delegate fired when the query is complete

Whether or not we want to mark these users as important so that they stay in the cache, defaults to false. This should only be used for users that we want to persist for the length of the game session, such as friends.

memberdef.argsstring: (const FUniqueNetId &UserId, const TArray< FString > &AccelByteIds, const FOnQueryUsersComplete &Delegate, bool bIsImportant=false)
memberdef.name: QueryUsersByAccelByteIds

detaileddescription.para: Queries all of the IDs listed in the array on the AccelByte backend for user information, including platform IDs. If platform IDs are found and match the current platform that we are on, extra queries will be made through the platform OSSes.
Caches any results that we get from the backend, as well as will not query from the backend again if a duplicate is found.

parameterdescription.para: FUniqueNetId of the user that is attempting to query for users
Array of strings that represent an ID for a single user
Delegate fired when the query is complete

Whether or not we want to mark these users as important so that they stay in the cache, defaults to false. This should only be used for users that we want to persist for the length of the game session, such as friends.

memberdef.argsstring: (const FUniqueNetId &UserId, const FString &PlatformType, const TArray< FString > &PlatformIds, const FOnQueryUsersComplete &Delegate, bool bIsImportant=false)
memberdef.name: QueryUsersByPlatformIds

detaileddescription.para: Tries to query all platform IDs listed for the particular platform specified on the AccelByte backend to find AccelByte user matches. If a matches are found, a subsequent query and cache will be performed to get information from the AccelByte backend on those users.

parameterdescription.para: FUniqueNetId of the user that is attempting to query for users
String representing the type of platform that these IDs belong to
Array of strings that represent an ID for a single user
Delegate fired when the query is complete

Whether or not we want to mark these users as important so that they stay in the cache, defaults to false. This should only be used for users that we want to persist for the length of the game session, such as friends.

detaileddescription.para: Attempt to get a user from the cache by an AccelByte unique ID. This ID comes from either an FAccelByteUserInfo::Id field, or from the result of a query users call.
Attempt to get a user from the cache by a composite ID structure.
Add an array of freshly queried users to the user cache
Add PublicCode to a user cache, create new if not exist
Add PublicCode to a user cache, create new if not exist

Searches through the user caches for a user that hasn't been accessed in longer than the maximum time set for this cache. If a user is found that exceeds this max time, and they are not marked as important, they will be removed from the cache entirely.
Will return the number of users purged from the cache.
Do not call this method directly, it will be called from the owning OnlineSubsystem's ticker!
Check whether a user exists already in the cache so that we don't requery them.

memberdef.argsstring: (const TArray< FString > &AccelByteIds, TArray< FString > &UsersToQuery, TArray< TSharedRef< FAccelByteUserInfo > > &UsersInCache)
memberdef.name: GetQueryAndCacheArrays

detaileddescription.para: Method used by user queries to get an array of users that we still need to query, as well as shared instances to users that we have already queried and can retrieve from the cache
Default constructor deleted, as we only want to be able to have an instance owned by a subsystem
Internal convenience method to convert platform strings to a key for the PlatformIdToUserInfoMap.

Manages users that are queried from the AccelByte backend, making bulk calls to retrieve user data, as well as getting extra necessary information for those users, such as platform IDs relevant to the current native platform.

As an explanation of how this works under the hood, the cache is made of shared FAccelByteUserInfo instances that are put into two maps. One map is for mapping the AccelByte ID to the user's information, and one is for mapping platform information to the user's information. This way either can be queried seamlessly to get the same user data. The only time you won't be able to query a user by their platform IDs is if they are not on the same platform as you, in which you can only query by AccelByte ID.

User data will be kept cached based on how long it has been since they have been accessed. You can configure how long users will stay in cache with the UserCachePurgeTimeoutSeconds variable in the OnlineSubsystemAccelByte settings in DefaultEngine.ini. Users will also not be purged if they were marked as important when queried.
